title: Bose — Strategic Leadership Architecture (Phase 1: Foundation)

concepts.title: Symbolic Decision-Making
concepts.description: making a choice matter through visibility; the method becomes the message; commitment through public gesture

concepts.title: Self-Sacrificial Signaling
concepts.description: using body or deprivation as credible proof of commitment; converting formal hierarchy into earned trust

concepts.title: Divine Attribution
concepts.description: legitimacy shortcut claiming supernatural mandate; unfalsifiable claim creating psychological permission

concepts.title: Mass Wedding Integration Theater
concepts.description: ritual kinship dissolving political boundaries; marriage as category collapse

concepts.title: Forward Base Logistics
concepts.description: supply infrastructure pre-positioned ahead of advance; conquered territory as productive supply hub

concepts.title: Deceptive Positioning
concepts.description: information warfare and enemy thought reorganization; winning before engagement

title: Bose — Strategic Leadership Architecture (Phase 2: Medium-Density Foundation)

concepts.title: The Mentorship Model
concepts.description: framework transmission from established genius to rising genius; intellectual osmosis and behavioral imprinting; dependency overcome through divergence

concepts.title: Accelerated Succession Management
concepts.description: rapid authority consolidation preventing opposition organization; early moves that can't be unmade; adaptation lag exploitation

concepts.title: Sacred Cows: Mythology as Political Architecture
concepts.description: myth as invisible enforcement; self-reinforcing feedback loops; narrative as operational tool; myth-reality divergence

concepts.title: Seven Leadership Styles
concepts.description: context-matched persona deployment; philosopher/warrior/administrator/marauder/visionary/strategist/god-king; audience resonance and style consistency

concepts.title: Global Strategy Under Constraints
concepts.description: expansion faster than consolidation; forward bases as force multipliers; elite integration; system collapse when motion stops; speed over durability tradeoff

title: READY HUBS

concepts.title: Behavioral Mechanics Hub
concepts.description: 41 pages (Hub A–G + BOM advanced); complete taxonomy of tactical psychology, tradecraft, and movement mechanics; source: Chase Hughes BOM + Wilson Movement/Machiavellian clusters

concepts.title: Behavioral Control & Psychological Manipulation
concepts.description: 13 pages (Hub I); foundational mechanisms of power accumulation through behavioral control; asymmetric vulnerability, reputation engineering, institutional hierarchy, psychological manipulation; source: Robert Greene 48 Laws of Power

concepts.title: Strategic Positioning & Indirect Approach
concepts.description: 5 pages: maneuvering opponent into self-defeat; backward planning from endpoint; timing and patience as force multiplier; strategic amorality; counterattack timing and emotional triggering

concepts.title: Power, Status & Hierarchy Performance
concepts.description: 5 pages: power and status as psychological/symbolic performance; status hierarchy as universal pattern; proximity to power sources; moral inversion in power dynamics; flattery as control mechanism

concepts.title: Pillars of Human Influence

concepts.description: Six-Minute X-Ray (6MX); Needs Map/Decision Map/Values Map; drive architecture

concepts.title: Social Resonance and Filters
concepts.description: group dynamics; Bystander effect; Unconscious Coherence; social permission

concepts.description: File Clerk metaphor; Confusion Levels 1–3; Lilac Protocol

concepts.description: 17 Needs taxonomy; 3 Selves model; Social Stability Scale

concepts.description: alter creation; entrainment scripts; the Organ Transplant procedure

concepts.title: The Belgrade Protocol and Wakeproofing
concepts.description: Operative Levels 4–7; MCR installation; wakeproofing architecture

concepts.description: cumulative/spontaneous amnesia; Wipers; The Voice

concepts.description: biological priming; hardware gear; environmental engineering

concepts.description: 12-point threshold; B-D-A timing; cluster analysis

concepts.description: field manual hub for the full Ellipsis corpus; master reference for TEM sequence

concepts.title: AVERY AI Integration Protocol
concepts.description: 4-day system installing Internal AI for cognitive/behavioral optimization; Admin Access identity model

concepts.title: Frame Control and Prompt Architecture
concepts.description: 4 Corners of a Frame; winning social frames (Father/Detached); high-stability AI context setting

concepts.title: Weaponized Cognitive Biases for Prompting
concepts.description: Anchoring/Zeigarnik/Pseudocertainty exploits; bypassing LLM statistical defaults

title: Chase Hughes Corpus — Synthesis

concepts.title: Tactical Influence Playbook
concepts.description: operational synthesis across BOM corpus; field-ready influence protocols

title: Wilson — Movement Mechanics (Hub F)

concepts.title: Public Dominance Architecture
concepts.description: layered environmental staging before a word is spoken; SA/machine gun/shoulder-through/elevation sequence

concepts.title: Crowd Turn and Conviction Contagion
concepts.description: Von Müller: "turned inside out as one turns a glove"; conviction transmits below language threshold

concepts.title: Linguistic Crowd Sedation
concepts.description: Göring's "keep drinking" move: threat redirection + comfort anchor + social permission for inaction

concepts.title: Theory of Victory
concepts.description: a plan requires causal chain A→Z; the Beer Hall had start and endpoint with no middle

concepts.title: Main Character Theory
concepts.description: claim responsibility to own the narrative; refuse positions where someone else writes your outcomes

concepts.title: Key Objective Discipline
concepts.description: identify the structural unlock position; refuse every substitute regardless of prestige

concepts.title: Dilatory Pivot
concepts.description: evaporate under force; let the opponent's attack spend itself on empty air; pivot when conditions shift

concepts.title: Defector Recapture
concepts.description: five-step Bear Hug: detect drift → private recognition → underlying need → self-authored public recommitment → elevate

title: Wilson — Machiavellian Mechanics (Hub G)

concepts.title: Virality Architecture
concepts.description: three conditions: edgy/taboo + succinct enough to spread + targets the vanguard not the masses

concepts.title: Front-Loaded Cruelty
concepts.description: "cruelties well used": concentrated at outset, then genuinely converted to benefits; Borgia/Remirro de Orco case

concepts.title: Loved, Feared, Hated Triad
concepts.description: three positions with structural logic; incoherence not severity is primary hatred generator

concepts.title: Enemy Management
concepts.description: harm-based enemies (must eliminate entirely) vs. fear-based opponents (can convert to strongest allies)

concepts.title: Machiavellian Dissimulation
concepts.description: virtue as separate performance layer from action; managing the gap between what you do and what people see

concepts.title: Self-Reliance Doctrine
concepts.description: never outsource core function; mercenaries give slow conquests and sudden astonishing losses

concepts.title: Initiative Reward Doctrine
concepts.description: punish failure of nerve, not failure of attempt; Romans rewarded commanders for failed operations if they tried

title: Greene — Behavioral Control & Psychological Manipulation (Hub I)

concepts.title: Asymmetric Vulnerability as Power Foundation
concepts.description: Power flows to those who suppress emotion while exploiting others' emotional needs. This asymmetry is the engine of behavioral control

concepts.title: Reputation as Controllable Fiction
concepts.description: Reputation functions independently from reality. Visibility management and narrative control allow reputation engineering independent of actual capability

concepts.title: The Master-Apprentice Hierarchy and Suppressed Brilliance
concepts.description: Institutional power requires suppression of subordinate brilliance. Visibility of competence in subordinates threatens hierarchical stability

concepts.title: Concealment and Strategic Opacity
concepts.description: Hiding intention while remaining sensitive to others' intentions creates informational asymmetry. Opacity allows prediction of others while preventing prediction of self

concepts.title: Information Control and Opacity Networks
concepts.description: Person who receives and distributes information first accumulates power asymmetrically. Information channels determine decision-making

concepts.title: Selective Honesty as Control Mechanism
concepts.description: Truthfulness in non-strategic domains builds perception of honesty that extends to strategic domains where deception operates

concepts.title: Sacrifice as Social Currency
concepts.description: Visible sacrifice creates psychological indebtedness that can be called in later. Converts cost into leverage and loyalty

concepts.title: Envy Management and the Visibility of Success
concepts.description: Visible success generates envy that produces opposition. Managing visibility prevents the envy that would otherwise create enemies

concepts.title: The Sucker Principle: Predation Through False Equality
concepts.description: False equality lowers mark's defenses. Predation operates through appearance of mutual relationship

concepts.title: Authority Construction and the Architecture of Belief
concepts.description: Authority is performed through symbols and positioning. Observers grant deference to those who perform authority convincingly

concepts.title: Boldness as Psychological Dominance
concepts.description: Bold action produces psychological effect that hesitation cannot match. Confidence is interpreted as knowledge

concepts.title: The Performance of Power
concepts.description: Power is performed through body language, voice, symbols, response patterns. Performance affects psychological terrain and actual power outcomes

concepts.title: Indirect Agency and Deniable Power
concepts.description: Power exercised through intermediaries remains invisible and unattributed. Allows power accumulation without opposition

concepts.title: Transgression and Concealment
concepts.description: Power accumulates to those willing to break rules while appearing to follow them. Hidden transgression through gaps and intermediaries

concepts.title: Crushing Enemies Completely and Utter Elimination
concepts.description: Partial elimination creates future conflict; complete elimination removes threat but is costly and creates victim narratives

concepts.title: Effortless Mastery and Hidden Labor
concepts.description: Hiding work that produces mastery allows observers to attribute success to natural talent rather than effort. Enhances reputation

concepts.title: Moral Inversion in Power Dynamics
concepts.description: Morality inverts in power contexts. Actions condemned in peer relationships reframed as pragmatism in hierarchical contexts

concepts.title: Status Hierarchy and the Pecking Order
concepts.description: Every group develops implicit status hierarchy. Movement within hierarchy requires visible capability, strategic positioning, and alliance-building

concepts.title: The Proximity Principle: Distance and Access as Power
concepts.description: Physical/informational proximity to power sources determines influence. Controlling access to power is power itself

concepts.title: Timing, Patience, and Strategic Delay
concepts.description: Right move at wrong time fails; wrong move at right time succeeds. Patience and timing often more important than the move itself

concepts.title: The Use of Flattery and Strategic Appreciation
concepts.description: Strategic appreciation builds loyalty through psychological mechanisms. Effective when genuine, targeted, and rare

title: Greene — Strategic Positioning and Long-Scale Dynamics (Hub H)

concepts.title: Strategic Amorality: Operating Outside Moral Frames
concepts.description: strategy as amoral mechanism; ethics and effectiveness as orthogonal dimensions

concepts.title: Information Asymmetry and Intelligence Dominance
concepts.description: win through knowing more; intelligence as primary strategic lever

concepts.title: Positioning Strategy: Make Enemies Defeat Themselves
concepts.description: structure situation so opponent loses through own choices

concepts.title: Weakness Revelation as Tactical Asset
concepts.description: controlled vulnerability as manipulation mechanism

concepts.title: Groupthink Exploitation and Social Conformity Leverage
concepts.description: shaping perceived consensus to drive conformity

concepts.title: Absence and Withdrawal as Active Strategy
concepts.description: not acting as strategic tool; withdrawal increases power through mystique

concepts.title: Environmental Mastery and Overwhelming Force
concepts.description: control space, control outcome; environment as weapon

concepts.title: The Backward Plan: Planning from Endpoint
concepts.description: work backward from desired outcome; identify prerequisites and causal chains

concepts.title: The Counterattack and Emotional Triggering
concepts.description: use opponent's emotional reaction as weapon

concepts.title: Epistemic Discipline and Noise Filtering
concepts.description: distinguish signal from noise; ignore what doesn't matter

concepts.title: Selective Honesty and Micro-Revelations
concepts.description: deploy truth strategically; reveal weakness to conceal advantage

title: Shotton — Consumer Behavior & Brand Strategy (HIGH & MEDIUM)

concepts.title: Expectation Assimilation
concepts.description: set expectations through experience sequence before product encounter; controls reference points through reveal timing

concepts.title: Scarcity Bias
concepts.description: limitation triggers urgency through time limits, quantity limits, or access restrictions; 33% redemption at 3 weeks vs 6% at 2 months

concepts.title: Nostalgia
concepts.description: memory activation premium pricing; willingness-to-pay triples for nostalgic framing vs non-nostalgic identical product

concepts.title: Trigger Moments & Implementation Intentions
concepts.description: if-then pairing automates behavior; 91% completion with trigger vs 42% intention-only

concepts.title: Sunk Cost Fallacy
concepts.description: past spending controls future decisions; $15 ticket → 4.11 shows vs $8 ticket → 3.29 shows (25% more attendance)

concepts.title: Uncertain Rewards & Variable Reinforcement
concepts.description: variable ratio (50% reward) generates 2x more behavior than fixed ratio (100% reward)

concepts.title: Near-Miss Effect
concepts.description: almost-winning drives more behavior than actual winning; near-win triggers more unrelated spending than actual wins

concepts.title: Goal Dilution Effect
concepts.description: single-benefit claims outsell multi-benefit 12%; specialization signals credibility

concepts.title: Pareidolia
concepts.description: face-like objects get 92% attention at 0.5s vs 80% non-face; logos with eyes grab attention faster

concepts.title: Habituation & Interruption
concepts.description: interrupted pleasure +17% satisfaction; seasonal products reset habituation

concepts.title: Incongruity & Humor
concepts.description: humor ads memorable but less persuasive; Snickers integrates humor with benefit

concepts.title: Concreteness Bias
concepts.description: concrete claims ("36% more energy") recalled 36% vs abstract ("improves energy") 9%

concepts.title: Optimal Newness (MAYA)
concepts.description: balance novelty with familiarity; skeuomorphism scaffolds novel features

concepts.title: Behavioral Residue
concepts.description: visible consumption traces signal identity; distinctive packaging creates residue

concepts.title: Operational Transparency
concepts.description: open kitchen +20% satisfaction, +15% willingness to pay; visible work justifies premium

concepts.title: Illusion of Progress
concepts.description: pre-filled loyalty cards accelerate completion 19%; illusion shortens timelines

concepts.title: Make It Easy
concepts.description: friction removal doubles behavior; dyed chip every 7th reduces consumption 50%

title: Evolutionary Psychology Mechanisms (Wright, 2026-04-24)

concepts.title: Ancestral Environment (EEA)
concepts.description: Environment of Evolutionary Adaptedness; the Pleistocene context producing human psychological mechanisms; mismatch between ancestral and modern environments as source of dysfunction (obesity, anxiety, status competition intensity)

concepts.title: Frequency-Dependent Selection
concepts.description: strategy fitness depends on how common it is in the population; when rare, cheating is profitable; when common, honesty is profitable; equilibrium point produces mixed strategy stable at population level

concepts.title: Behavioral Plasticity
concepts.description: capacity to adjust strategy based on local conditions; developmental window producing different adaptive responses in different environments; same genes, different phenotypes

concepts.title: Trivers-Willard Hypothesis
concepts.description: parental investment allocation shifts based on parental quality and offspring sex-specific payoffs; high-quality parents bias toward costly sex; low-quality parents toward fecund sex

concepts.title: Reproductive Value
concepts.description: how much future reproduction an organism can expect; peaks at reproductive years; shapes parental investment allocation, mate choice, and risk-taking

concepts.title: Non-Zero-Sum Cooperation
concepts.description: repeated interactions creating surplus; makes cheating costly and cooperation profitable; iterated prisoner's dilemma logic in human relationships

concepts.title: Deception Detection
concepts.description: cognitive specialization for identifying cheaters in reciprocal altruism; logic-puzzle performance jumps when framed as detecting cheaters; content-specific reasoning

concepts.title: Parental Investment Trade-Offs
concepts.description: mating effort vs. parental effort; number vs. quality of offspring; current vs. future reproduction; trade-offs shape risk-taking, mate-guarding, and sexual jealousy intensity

title: Bernays / PR Manipulation and Social Engineering

concepts.title: Propaganda as Social Technology
concepts.description: propaganda as neutral transmission mechanism; six-step operational sequence; public interest problem / mechanism-contradicts-goal paradox

concepts.title: Engineering of Consent
concepts.description: eight-step named methodology (goals → research → reorient → strategy → organize → time → create news → budget); Light's Golden Jubilee and Coolidge breakfast as evidence

concepts.title: Manufactured Event / Overt Act Theory
concepts.description: five structural requirements; Will Irwin news definition; PR counsel as creator not purveyor of news; Easter Parade / Green Ball case roster

concepts.title: Intelligent Minority Doctrine
concepts.description: three-component structure (epistemic/organizational/legitimating); failure of legitimating claim (Guatemala, Goebbels); democratic contradiction

concepts.title: PR Counsel as Profession
concepts.description: legal analogy; "judge and jury in public opinion" framing; four competencies; self-legitimating ethics problem

concepts.title: Attitude Polling as Research Foundation
concepts.description: research as Step 2 of engineering-of-consent; terrain map vs. social force dual function; measurement and manufacturing use same instrument

concepts.title: Democracy Defense via Propaganda
concepts.description: inoculation paradox (mechanism identical to what it defends against); three-part prescription; Goebbels parallel acknowledged

concepts.title: MacDougall Instinct Catalog
concepts.description: seven primary instincts with PR applications (flight/repulsion/curiosity/pugnacity/self-display/self-abasement/parental); four additional instincts; decay problem

concepts.title: Interlapping Group Formations
concepts.description: individuals belong to multiple overlapping groups; three consequences (multi-channel appeal, group leader leverage, gradual opinion change); silk company / Russian Ballet evidence

title: Shotton — Consumer Behavior MEDIUM-Density Pages

concepts.title: Temporal Reframing: Pennies a Day
concepts.description: £350/year vs £1/day; 53% adoption difference from temporal framing alone; same cost, different perception

concepts.title: Descriptive Language
concepts.description: "Smashed" 40.5mph vs "contacted" 31.8mph (27% difference for identical accident); language shapes memory encoding

concepts.title: Costly Signaling
concepts.description: £20M advertising spend rated 6.16/9 quality vs £2M spend 5.39/9 (14% higher); waste signals authenticity

concepts.title: Price Relativity
concepts.description: Ben & Jerry's perceived 27% value in Walmart vs 41% in Whole Foods; same price, different perceived value based on anchors

concepts.title: Price as Quality Badge
concepts.description: £45 wine activates reward brain 70% more than £5 identical wine; price signals quality; perception changes actual experience

concepts.title: Foreign Branding
concepts.description: foreign origin signals quality through scarcity + cultural association; Häagen-Dazs invented Danish name despite Brooklyn origin

concepts.title: Information Gap and Curiosity
concepts.description: gap between known and desired knowledge triggers curiosity-driven motivation; KFC secret recipe maintains gap indefinitely

concepts.title: Zeigarnik Effect
concepts.description: unfinished tasks 90% better recalled; unfinished ads 34% better immediate recall, 52% better 2-day recall

concepts.title: Regret Lottery
concepts.description: potential regret motivates 26% more engagement than fixed rewards; loss emotion stronger than reward emotion

concepts.title: False Scarcity
concepts.description: artificial limits trigger real behavior change; "Limit 12" sign increases consumption 25%; "Sold Out" signals +21.8% value

concepts.title: Keats Heuristic and Rhyming
concepts.description: rhyming statements perceived 17% more true; 25% more memorable, 24% more likable, 22% more trustworthy

concepts.title: Skeuomorphism
concepts.description: digital interfaces feel familiar when designed with physical metaphors; leather, wood, torn paper reduce cognitive load

concepts.title: Fluent Devices and Mascots
concepts.description: recognizable mascots increase brand share 8-15%; GEICO Gecko drove 12% market share growth 1999-2009

concepts.title: Synesthesia and Multi-Sensory Branding
concepts.description: coordinated sensory inputs integrate into unified quality perception; heavy fork improves food taste 20%

title: Shotton — Consumer Behavior LOW-Density Pages

concepts.title: Charm Pricing
concepts.description: $4.99 vs $5.00: 9% higher sales; left-digit bias anchors perception despite 2-cent difference

concepts.title: Stolen Thunder Technique
concepts.description: preemptive disclosure reduces guilt perception 12%; increases credibility 15%; frames weakness as honesty

concepts.title: Sold Out Framing
concepts.description: "Sold Out" triggers 8% disappointment vs "Out of Stock" 15%; demand-driven scarcity signals value

concepts.title: Averted Gaze
concepts.description: model looking away pulls attention toward gazed object 58% more; gaze-following hijacks visual attention system
